title: checkpoint/restore fails with PostgreSQL
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Container checkpoint fails for PostgreSQL without the CRIU fix in https://github.com/checkpoint-restore/criu/pull/1622.
However, with this fix container restore still fails due to missing files in `/dev/shm` 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo podman  run --name some-postgres -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=mysecretpassword -d postgres`

2. `sudo podman container checkpoint some-postgres`

3. `sudo podman container restore some-postgres`

**Describe the results you received:**
Podman error message:

> Error: OCI runtime error: CRIU restoring failed -52.  Please check CRIU logfile /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/eed25ae4295e9b5f5af50adfca4c910e89baa0fab751f5b23be1e4ce520d2763/userdata/restore.log


CRIU error message:
```
    56: Opening 0x007f5b8d13b000-0x007f5b8d142000 0000000000000000 (81) vma
    56: Error (criu/files-reg.c:2143): Can't open file dev/shm/PostgreSQL.2016670368 on restore: No such file or directory
     1: Opening 0x007f5b8d13b000-0x007f5b8d142000 0000000000000000 (81) vma
    56: Error (criu/files-reg.c:2086): Can't open file dev/shm/PostgreSQL.2016670368: No such file or directory
    56: Error (criu/mem.c:1349): `- Can't open vma
     1: Error (criu/files-reg.c:2143): Can't open file dev/shm/PostgreSQL.2016670368 on restore: No such file or directory

    58: Opening 0x007f5b8d13b000-0x007f5b8d142000 0000000000000000 (81) vma
    58: Error (criu/files-reg.c:2143): Can't open file dev/shm/PostgreSQL.2016670368 on restore: No such file or directory

    58: Error (criu/mem.c:1349): `- Can't open vma
    57: Error (criu/files-reg.c:2143): Can't open file dev/shm/PostgreSQL.2016670368 on restore: No such file or directory
    59: Opening 0x007f5b8d13b000-0x007f5b8d142000 0000000000000000 (81) vma
    57: Error (criu/files-reg.c:2086): Can't open file dev/shm/PostgreSQL.2016670368: No such file or directory
    57: Error (criu/mem.c:1349): `- Can't open vma
    61: Opening 0x007f5b8d127000-0x007f5b8d12a000 0000000000000000 (20000041) vma
    59: Error (criu/files-reg.c:2143): Can't open file dev/shm/PostgreSQL.2016670368 on restore: No such file or directory
    59: Error (criu/files-reg.c:2086): Can't open file dev/shm/PostgreSQL.2016670368: No such file or directory

    61: Error (criu/files-reg.c:2143): Can't open file dev/shm/PostgreSQL.2016670368 on restore: No such file or directory
    61: Error (criu/files-reg.c:2086): Can't open file dev/shm/PostgreSQL.2016670368: No such file or directory
    61: Error (criu/mem.c:1349): `- Can't open vma
mnt: Switching to new ns to clean ghosts
Error (criu/cr-restore.c:2470): Restoring FAILED. 
```
[restore.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/7526782/restore.log.txt)

**Describe the results you expected:**
Successful container restore

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

```

comments.body: Can you provide the logfile it mentioned?

Ah, nevermind, I see it now.

@adrianreber PTAL
For reference, `/dev/shm` is a tmpfs in a the container, so I imagine that's related.

I already discussed it with @rst0git

> For reference, `/dev/shm` is a tmpfs in a the container, so I imagine that's related.

From what I have seen that is not entirely true. `/dev/shm` is a tmpfs on the outside of the container and bind mounted into the container. A tmpfs in the container would be easy, but a tmpfs outside of the container is not handled by CRIU.

The solution we came up with for exporting checkpoints is basically:
 * During checkpointing, before unmounting the tmpfs we store everything in the tmpfs in the export archive
 * During restore we copy the saved content from the checkpoint archive to the tmpfs before restoring the container

For checkpoints which are not exported we have a similar problem. After checkpointing we unmount the tmpfs and everything from `/dev/shm` is gone. So we also have to copy the content of the tmpfs somewhere before unmounting. We already have a location for the actual checkpoint data, so maybe we just put it next to it.

For both cases the solution is not really difficult and I think @rst0git agreed to implement it, right?

I wonder if there's a good reason for us to have this special handling for `/dev/shm` - I'll bring this up at our scrum, but I don't see a specific reason why it needs to be a tmpfs that is subsequently mounted in.

> I wonder if there's a good reason for us to have this special handling for `/dev/shm` - I'll bring this up at our scrum, but I don't see a specific reason why it needs to be a tmpfs that is subsequently mounted in.

We were also wondering why this is the case. For a pod with a shared IPC namespace it makes sense, but for a standalone container it was not obvious to us.

> For both cases the solution is not really difficult and I think @rst0git agreed to implement it, right?

Yes, I hope to open a PR with a fix soon.

The /dev/shm is for sharing IPC namespace with another container.  The issue is you never know whether an existing container will be shared.  So we had to mount it to container storage and then bind mount it into the container.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@adrianreber I have a patch for this issue but it requires the changes in https://github.com/checkpoint-restore/criu/pull/1622
